task_completion.description: Verify that a support ticket from Alice Chen is open in the helpdesk interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The helpdesk UI shows an open conversation with the customer email 'alice.chen@example.org' or name 'Alice Chen'.

task_completion.description: Verify the presence of the first original message in the consolidated ticket.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The conversation history contains a message block with the text 'checking on my shipment'.

task_completion.description: Verify the presence of the second original message in the consolidated ticket.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The conversation history contains a message block with the text 'wrong zip code'.

task_completion.description: Verify the presence of the third original message in the consolidated ticket.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The conversation history contains a message block with the text 'apartment number 4B'.

task_completion.description: Verify that all three messages have been successfully merged into this single ticket.
task_completion.visual_evidence: All three distinct messages appear within the timeline of the SAME ticket thread. There may also be a system event message in the timeline indicating that conversations were merged.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the helpdesk web interface to perform the merge.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show a web browser open to the helpdesk application (e.g., FreeScout), not a terminal, API client, or database tool.

integrity.description: Verify the messages were actually merged and not just copy-pasted into a new reply or note.
integrity.visual_evidence: The messages appear as distinct historical email blocks in the thread (often with their own headers/timestamps), rather than being manually typed or pasted into a single reply box or internal note.

integrity.description: Verify the result is not mocked in a text editor or static HTML file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface is a live, functional helpdesk web application with standard navigation menus, not a text editor like VS Code or Notepad.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Customer Email: alice.chen@example.org. The three original messages to be merged contain the following specific phrases: 1) 'checking on my shipment', 2) 'wrong zip code', 3) 'apartment number 4B'. A successful merge means all three of these distinct messages will appear in the history of a single ticket thread.
